title: tetrastigma pedunculare
intro: Tetrastigma pedunculare, also known as the Chinese grapevine or Five-Leaf Grapevine, is a species of plant in the family Vitaceae. It is a woody climbing vine native to southern China, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and stems are valued for their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, and are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Tetrastigma pedunculare is often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that promote overall health and well-being, and are used to boost the immune system and reduce stress.
